title: For most US drivers, EVs offer emissions benefits and cost savings
excerpt: In most U.S. zip codes, electric vehicles are cost-competitive with their gas-powered counterparts, according to a new study. And regarding emissions benefits of EVs, individual driving patterns matter as much as regional factors like the local electricity mix.
content_html:
Despite regional variability in climate, electricity sources, congestion, and the wide variation in individual driving patterns, electric vehicles generate less greenhouse gas emissions and do not cost more than comparable gas-powered vehicles for drivers and vehicle fleet owners in most parts of the United States, according to a new study by MIT researchers.

The team’s approach captures many key factors that contribute to regional and individual differences in the life-cycle emissions and ownership cost of electric vehicles, including meteorological data, the distance and duration of trips, and fuel prices.

To paint a fuller picture of emissions and costs than was previously available, the researchers sourced data from thousands of U.S. zip codes and drilled down to the level of individual drivers within those locations. Their study considers time-averaged fuel prices so as not to be overly influenced by fluctuations in prices at any one point in time. They finalized their analysis at the end of 2024 and early 2025.

Their results indicate that a person’s driving behaviors can matter as much as regional factors like the local electricity mix when it comes to the emissions savings of an electric vehicle, compared to a similar gas-powered vehicle. In most locations, a battery-electric vehicle reduces emissions between 40 and 60 percent, with larger impacts in urban areas. 

They also found that colder climates do not reduce overall emission benefits as much as some media reports assume.

The researchers utilized this detailed analysis to update a public tool they previously developed, carboncounter.com, which enables individuals to compare the life-cycle emissions and total ownership costs of nearly any car on the market. A new version of carboncounter.com is also being released today.

“There are a lot of statements being thrown around, like that electric vehicles don’t reduce emissions very much in cool climates, and we wanted to analyze these factors systematically and evaluate these statements against one another simultaneously. Rather than simply asking, ‘Are EVs better?’, this paper helps answer ‘better for whom, and under what conditions?’” says Marco Miotti PhD ’20, a senior researcher at ETH Zurich who completed this research while a graduate student in the Institute for Data, Systems, and Society (IDSS) at MIT. 

He is joined on the paper by senior author Jessika Trancik, a professor in IDSS. The research appears today in Environmental Research Letters.

A holistic approach

Many prior studies that compare emissions and costs of electric vehicles (EVs) to combustion-engine vehicles cover a few factors, like the amount of renewable energy in the grid and how gas prices impact affordability, Miotti says.

“To our knowledge, there have been few efforts so far that bring all these factors together. But if someone wants to buy a car and have a better understanding of the factors that affect emissions and costs, this holistic approach is important,” he adds.

The researchers focused on two types of EVs: battery-electric vehicles, which only operate on electricity, and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles, which also have a combustion engine that works in tandem with the battery to optimize fuel savings.

The team expanded and improved a set of previously developed vehicle cost and emissions models to incorporate a wider variety of factors and data types.

For instance, they refined an existing model that estimates energy use and gas mileage so it could capture more nuances of local climate variability. 

“But the real effort was not just in extending these different models, but in bringing together all these different data and making them work with the models in a consistent manner,” Miotti says.

The team sourced data on a wide variety of factors for each U.S. zip code, such as typical drive cycles, the amount of traffic, local gas and electricity prices, makeup of the regional electricity mix, meteorological profiles, and more. They used statistical approaches to amalgamate different types of data. 

For example, the team used a probabilistic matching technique to combine data on how often people drive, which was drawn from nationwide travel surveys, with more detailed GPS data that includes factors like drivers’ acceleration patterns and the distance they usually drive on each day of the week.

The researchers designed their analysis to focus on the spatial picture of emissions and costs, based on U.S. zip codes, while simultaneously considering the impact of the size and features of each specific vehicle model.

“At the end of the day, it’s the vehicle and fleet owners who make decisions about vehicle purchases. So, we wanted to make sure to consider their wide-ranging individual perspectives rather than simply performing a region-by-region comparison,” says Trancik.

Lower emissions, comparable costs

In the end, their modeling framework revealed that all factors they analyzed matter about equally in determining emissions-reduction potential of EVs compared to internal combustion vehicles. 

EVs reduce emissions the most in areas with a cleaner electricity mix, denser traffic, higher annual travel distances, and a mild climate, in decreasing order of importance. In each area, emission reductions increase for drivers who drive more often, drive larger vehicles, and are more frequently stuck in traffic. 

In a colder area like North Dakota, fuel economy of battery-electric vehicles might be reduced by as much as 50 percent on a particularly frigid night, but the effect on annual emission benefits is minimal. 

“We even did a sensitivity study to see if the range is reduced in very cold climates, and we found that, even in the most unfavorable conditions, EVs still reduce emissions by a substantial amount,” Miotti says.

On the cost side, the models show that, in most places across the U.S., EVs are competitive with comparable combustion-engine vehicles in terms of lifetime ownership cost, even without clean vehicle tax credits. And in areas where electricity is relatively affordable, battery-electric vehicles tend to cost less than their plug-in hybrid or combustion-engine counterparts.

In the future, the researchers want to expand this analysis to include a temporal dimension, so the framework also considers how changes in vehicle, fuel, and electricity prices affect emissions and costs over time. 

“While we found that the electricity mix is a big driver of the spatial variation in emissions savings of EVs, the electricity grid is decarbonizing everywhere. As that happens, emissions savings across space will become more homogenous for EVs, but the differences across one driver to another will remain,” Miotti says.

They could also use the framework to explore regions outside the United States or incorporate data on hybrid-electric vehicles that cannot be plugged in.

This work was funded, in part, by the MIT Martin Family Society of Fellows for Sustainability.

Press Mentions

National Public Radio (NPR)

NPR reporter Jeff Brady spotlights a study by Prof. Jessika Trancik and Marco Miotti PhD ’20  that found “across most of the U.S., electric vehicles are cost-competitive with their gas counterparts. And it found that in most locations, EVs also reduce emissions between 40% and 60%.” 

Related Links

  • Carboncounter.com
  • Marco Miotti
  • Jessika Trancik
  • Trancik Lab
  • Institute for Data, Systems, and Society
  • School of Engineering
  • MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

blocks.html_en: Despite regional variability in climate, electricity sources, congestion, and the wide variation in individual driving patterns, electric vehicles generate less greenhouse gas emissions and do not cost more than comparable gas-powered vehicles for drivers and vehicle fleet owners in most parts of the United States, according to a new study by MIT researchers.

sentences.en: Despite regional variability in climate, electricity sources, congestion, and the wide variation in individual driving patterns, electric vehicles generate less greenhouse gas emissions and do not cost more than comparable gas-powered vehicles for drivers and vehicle fleet owners in most parts of the United States, according to a new study by MIT researchers.
sentences.zh: 麻省理工学院研究人员的一项新研究表明，尽管气候、电力来源、交通拥堵以及个人驾驶模式存在巨大差异，但电动汽车产生的温室气体排放较少，而且对于美国大部分地区的司机和车队所有者来说，电动汽车的成本并不高于同类汽油动力汽车。

blocks.html_en: The team’s approach captures many key factors that contribute to regional and individual differences in the life-cycle emissions and ownership cost of electric vehicles, including meteorological data, the distance and duration of trips, and fuel prices.

blocks.html_en: To paint a fuller picture of emissions and costs than was previously available, the researchers sourced data from thousands of U.S. zip codes and drilled down to the level of individual drivers within those locations. Their study considers time-averaged fuel prices so as not to be overly influenced by fluctuations in prices at any one point in time. They finalized their analysis at the end of 2024 and early 2025.

sentences.en: Their study considers time-averaged fuel prices so as not to be overly influenced by fluctuations in prices at any one point in time.
sentences.zh: 他们的研究考虑了时间平均燃料价格，以免受到任何一个时间点价格波动的过度影响。

sentences.en: They finalized their analysis at the end of 2024 and early 2025.

sentences.en: In most locations, a battery-electric vehicle reduces emissions between 40 and 60 percent, with larger impacts in urban areas.
sentences.zh: 在大多数地方，电动汽车可减少 40% 至 60% 的排放，其中对城市地区的影响更大。

blocks.html_en: “We even did a sensitivity study to see if the range is reduced in very cold climates, and we found that, even in the most unfavorable conditions, EVs still reduce emissions by a substantial amount,” Miotti says.

sentences.en: “We even did a sensitivity study to see if the range is reduced in very cold climates, and we found that, even in the most unfavorable conditions, EVs still reduce emissions by a substantial amount,” Miotti says.
sentences.zh: “我们甚至进行了一项敏感性研究，看看在非常寒冷的气候下续航里程是否会减少，我们发现，即使在最不利的条件下，电动汽车仍然可以大量减少排放，”米奥蒂说。

blocks.html_en: On the cost side, the models show that, in most places across the U.S., EVs are competitive with comparable combustion-engine vehicles in terms of lifetime ownership cost, even without clean vehicle tax credits. And in areas where electricity is relatively affordable, battery-electric vehicles tend to cost less than their plug-in hybrid or combustion-engine counterparts.

sentences.en: On the cost side, the models show that, in most places across the U.S., EVs are competitive with comparable combustion-engine vehicles in terms of lifetime ownership cost, even without clean vehicle tax credits.
sentences.zh: 在成本方面，模型显示，在美国大多数地方，即使没有清洁车辆税收抵免，电动汽车在终身拥有成本方面也与同类内燃机汽车相比具有竞争力。

sentences.en: And in areas where electricity is relatively affordable, battery-electric vehicles tend to cost less than their plug-in hybrid or combustion-engine counterparts.
sentences.zh: 在电力相对便宜的地区，电池电动汽车的成本往往低于插电式混合动力或内燃机汽车。

blocks.html_en: In the future, the researchers want to expand this analysis to include a temporal dimension, so the framework also considers how changes in vehicle, fuel, and electricity prices affect emissions and costs over time.

sentences.en: In the future, the researchers want to expand this analysis to include a temporal dimension, so the framework also considers how changes in vehicle, fuel, and electricity prices affect emissions and costs over time.
sentences.zh: 未来，研究人员希望将这种分析扩展到时间维度，因此该框架还考虑了车辆、燃料和电力价格的变化如何随着时间的推移影响排放和成本。

blocks.html_en: “While we found that the electricity mix is a big driver of the spatial variation in emissions savings of EVs, the electricity grid is decarbonizing everywhere. As that happens, emissions savings across space will become more homogenous for EVs, but the differences across one driver to another will remain,” Miotti says.

sentences.en: “While we found that the electricity mix is a big driver of the spatial variation in emissions savings of EVs, the electricity grid is decarbonizing everywhere.
sentences.zh: “虽然我们发现电力结构是电动汽车减排空间变化的一个重要驱动因素，但各地的电网都在脱碳。

sentences.en: As that happens, emissions savings across space will become more homogenous for EVs, but the differences across one driver to another will remain,” Miotti says.
sentences.zh: 当这种情况发生时，电动汽车在各个空间的减排量将变得更加同质，但不同驾驶员之间的差异仍然存在，”米奥蒂说。

blocks.html_en: They could also use the framework to explore regions outside the United States or incorporate data on hybrid-electric vehicles that cannot be plugged in.

sentences.en: They could also use the framework to explore regions outside the United States or incorporate data on hybrid-electric vehicles that cannot be plugged in.
sentences.zh: 他们还可以使用该框架探索美国以外的地区，或整合有关无法插电的混合动力电动汽车的数据。

blocks.html_en: This work was funded, in part, by the MIT Martin Family Society of Fellows for Sustainability.

And it found that in most locations, EVs also reduce emissions between 40% and 60%.”

content_html_zh:
麻省理工学院研究人员的一项新研究表明，尽管气候、电力来源、交通拥堵以及个人驾驶模式存在巨大差异，但电动汽车产生的温室气体排放较少，而且对于美国大部分地区的司机和车队所有者来说，电动汽车的成本并不高于同类汽油动力汽车。

该团队的方法捕捉到了导致电动汽车生命周期排放和拥有成本的地区和个体差异的许多关键因素，包括气象数据、出行距离和持续时间以及燃油价格。

为了比以前更全面地了解排放和成本，研究人员从数千个美国邮政编码中获取了数据，并深入到这些地点的个体司机的水平。 他们的研究考虑了时间平均燃料价格，以免受到任何一个时间点价格波动的过度影响。 他们于 2024 年底和 2025 年初完成了分析。

他们的研究结果表明，与类似的燃气动力汽车相比，在电动汽车的减排方面，一个人的驾驶行为与当地电力结构等区域因素一样重要。 在大多数地方，电动汽车可减少 40% 至 60% 的排放，其中对城市地区的影响更大。

他们还发现，寒冷的气候并没有像一些媒体报道所假设的那样减少总体排放效益。

研究人员利用这一详细分析更新了他们之前开发的公共工具carboncounter.com，该工具使个人能够比较市场上几乎所有汽车的生命周期排放和总拥有成本。 Carboncounter.com 的新版本也于今天发布。

“有很多说法，比如电动汽车在凉爽气候下不会减少太多排放，我们希望系统地分析这些因素，并同时对这些说法进行评估。 这篇论文不是简单地问“电动汽车更好吗？”，而是帮助回答“对谁更好、在什么条件下更好？””苏黎世联邦理工学院高级研究员 Marco Miotti 博士 20 岁说，他在麻省理工学院数据、系统和社会研究所 (IDSS) 攻读研究生时完成了这项研究。

IDSS 教授、资深作者 Jessika Trancik 与他一起撰写了这篇论文。 该研究今天发表在《环境研究快报》上。

整体方法

米奥蒂说，许多先前的研究比较了电动汽车（EV）与内燃机汽车的排放和成本，涵盖了一些因素，例如电网中可再生能源的数量以及天然气价格如何影响可负担性。

“据我们所知，到目前为止，几乎没有什么努力将所有这些因素结合在一起。 但如果有人想购买汽车并更好地了解影响排放和成本的因素，这种整体方法就很重要，”他补充道。

研究人员重点研究了两种类型的电动汽车：仅靠电力运行的电池电动汽车和插电式混合动力电动汽车，后者还配有与电池协同工作的内燃机，以优化燃油节省。

该团队扩展并改进了一组先前开发的车辆成本和排放模型，以纳入更广泛的因素和数据类型。

例如，他们改进了估计能源使用和汽油里程的现有模型，以便能够捕捉当地气候变化的更多细微差别。

“但真正的努力不仅仅是扩展这些不同的模型，而是将所有这些不同的数据汇集在一起​​，并使它们以一致的方式与模型一起工作，”米奥蒂说。

该团队收集了美国每个邮政编码的各种因素的数据，例如典型的驾驶周期、交通量、当地天然气和电力价格、地区电力结构的构成、气象概况等等。 他们使用统计方法来合并不同类型的数据。

例如，该团队使用概率匹配技术，将来自全国旅行调查的人们开车频率的数据与更详细的 GPS 数据结合起来，其中包括驾驶员的加速模式和一周中每天通常行驶的距离等因素。

研究人员设计的分析重点是基于美国邮政编码的排放和成本的空间图，同时考虑每种特定车型的尺寸和特征的影响。

“归根结底，是车辆和车队所有者做出车辆购买决定。 因此，我们希望确保考虑他们广泛的个人观点，而不是简单地进行逐个地区的比较，”Trancik 说。

排放量更低，成本可比

最后，他们的建模框架显示，与内燃机汽车相比，他们分析的所有因素在确定电动汽车的减排潜力方面同样重要。

在电力结构更清洁、交通更密集、年行驶距离更长和气候温和的地区，电动汽车减少的排放量最多，按重要性递减顺序排列。 在每个地区，驾驶频率更高、驾驶大型车辆以及更频繁堵车的驾驶员的排放量都会增加。

在北达科他州等较寒冷的地区，在特别寒冷的夜晚，电动汽车的燃油经济性可能会降低多达 50%，但对年度排放效益的影响却很小。

“我们甚至进行了一项敏感性研究，看看在非常寒冷的气候下续航里程是否会减少，我们发现，即使在最不利的条件下，电动汽车仍然可以大量减少排放，”米奥蒂说。

在成本方面，模型显示，在美国大多数地方，即使没有清洁车辆税收抵免，电动汽车在终身拥有成本方面也与同类内燃机汽车相比具有竞争力。 在电力相对便宜的地区，电池电动汽车的成本往往低于插电式混合动力或内燃机汽车。

未来，研究人员希望将这种分析扩展到时间维度，因此该框架还考虑了车辆、燃料和电力价格的变化如何随着时间的推移影响排放和成本。

“虽然我们发现电力结构是电动汽车减排空间变化的一个重要驱动因素，但各地的电网都在脱碳。 当这种情况发生时，电动汽车在各个空间的减排量将变得更加同质，但不同驾驶员之间的差异仍然存在，”米奥蒂说。

他们还可以使用该框架探索美国以外的地区，或整合有关无法插电的混合动力电动汽车的数据。

这项工作的部分资金由麻省理工学院马丁家庭可持续发展研究员协会资助。

媒体提及

国家公共广播电台 (NPR)

NPR 记者 Jeff Brady 重点介绍了 Jessika Trancik 教授和 20 届博士 Marco Miotti 的一项研究，该研究发现“在美国大部分地区，电动汽车与天然气汽车相比具有成本竞争力。 研究发现，在大多数地方，电动汽车还可以减少 40% 到 60% 的排放量。”
